metadata.defaultDescription: In-person party games you can play with a single phone. No signup required.
metadata.aboutDescription: Learn about FunSpace — in-person party games for friends, playable on one shared phone.
metadata.playersDescription: Manage your saved player names for FunSpace party games.

home.heroTagline: Party games for friends, one phone
home.gamesSubheading: Pick a game and play together in the same room.

about.title: About FunSpace
about.description: FunSpace is a collection of in-person party games you can play with friends using a single shared phone. No accounts, no downloads — just open the site and play.

features.title: What we offer
features.catalog: A growing catalog of word-based and social games
features.singlePhone: Every game works on one shared device

impostor.name: Impostor
impostor.description: Find the impostor who doesn't know the secret word. Pass the phone to reveal roles privately.

hangman.name: Hangman
hangman.description: Guess the word letter by letter before the drawing is complete.

neverHaveIEver.name: Never Have I Ever
neverHaveIEver.description: Read prompts aloud and learn surprising things about your friends.

questionImpostor.name: Question Impostor
questionImpostor.description: Everyone gets a question — but the impostor's is different. Answer aloud and find who is faking it.

truthOrDare.name: Truth or Dare
truthOrDare.description: Pick Truth or Dare and let the app surface prompts — the group decides who answers or acts.

wouldYouRather.name: Would You Rather
wouldYouRather.description: Two options on screen — debate as a group and pick verbally. The app moderates, you decide.

shellDemo.name: Shell Demo
shellDemo.description: A minimal demo game to validate setup, reveal, play, and resolve.

gameShell.comingSoonMessage: This game is not playable yet. Check back soon!

presetTooltips.classic: 1 impostor, no spies. Best for learning the rules.
presetTooltips.withSpy: 1 impostor and 1 spy. The spy gets a similar word and wins with civilians.
presetTooltips.chaos: 2 impostors for bigger groups. Harder for civilians to survive!
presetTooltips.custom: Set your own impostor and spy counts.

validation.emptyRoster: Add players on the Players page before starting.
validation.noPacks: Select at least one word pack.
validation.noCivilians: Too many impostors and spies — at least one civilian is required.
validation.tooManyImpostors: Too many impostors for this player count.
validation.tooManySpies: Too many spies for this player count.

reveal.impostorMessage: You don't know the secret word. Blend in and survive.

play.instructions: Discuss and give clues. When the group decides someone is out, eliminate them here.

goal.title: Goal
goal.body: Most players know a secret word. One or more impostors don't. Give clues, discuss at the table, and figure out who is faking it before the impostors take over.

items.civilian: Civilian — knows the secret word and tries to find the impostors.
items.impostor: Impostor — does not know the word and must blend in. Impostors don't know each other.
items.spy: Spy (optional) — gets a similar decoy word and wins with the civilians.

steps.1: Choose players and options, then start the game.
steps.2: Pass the phone around — each player privately sees their role and word (or an impostor message).
steps.3: Discuss at the table and give verbal clues. There is no timer and voting happens in real life, not in the app.
steps.4: When the group agrees someone is out, eliminate them in the app. Their role stays hidden until the end.
steps.5: Keep playing until civilians eliminate all impostors, or only impostors remain alive.

items.civilians: Civilians (and any spy) win when all impostors have been eliminated.
items.impostors: Impostors win when only impostors are still alive.

setup.emptyRoster: Add players on the Players page before starting a game.
setup.playersSubtitle: Select {min}–{max} players from your saved roster.

presetTooltips.forgiving: 8 wrong guesses — a little more room for the group.
presetTooltips.custom: Choose your own max wrong guesses (4–10).

validation.emptyRoster: Add players on the Players page before starting.
validation.noPacks: Select at least one word pack.

play.instructions: Discuss as a group and tap letters on the phone. Wrong guesses build the hangman.

goal.title: Goal
goal.body: Guess the secret word letter by letter before the hangman drawing is complete. Everyone plays together on one phone.

items.group: The group — suggest letters aloud and agree on each guess together.
items.phone: The phone — picks a secret word, tracks guesses, and draws the hangman.

steps.1: Choose players, difficulty, and word packs, then start.
steps.2: The app picks a secret word and shows blanks.
steps.3: Discuss and tap letters — correct guesses reveal letters everywhere they appear.
steps.4: Keep guessing until you complete the word or run out of wrong guesses.

items.civilians: You win when every letter in the word has been guessed.
items.impostors: You lose when wrong guesses reach the limit and the word is still incomplete.

presetTooltips.icebreaker: Classic safe prompts only — great for new groups.
presetTooltips.mixed: Classic, Travel, and Food & Fun packs together.

spicyConfirm.message: The Spicy pack includes adult-themed prompts. Only continue if everyone in the group is 18+ and comfortable.

validation.emptyRoster: Add players on the Players page before starting.
validation.noPacks: Select at least one prompt pack.

play.instructions: Anyone who has done it responds in person — put a finger down, take a sip, or follow your house rules.

rules.title: How to play Never Have I Ever

goal.title: Goal
goal.body: Read prompts aloud and learn surprising things about your friends. The app shows prompts — the real game happens at the table.

items.group: The group — anyone who has done the prompt responds in person (fingers down, sips, or your house rules).
items.reader: The reader — reads each prompt aloud from the shared phone.

steps.1: Choose players and prompt packs, then start.
steps.2: Read the prompt on screen aloud to the group.
steps.3: Anyone who has done it responds in person — the app does not track scores.
steps.4: Tap Next for another prompt, Skip to move on, or End session when you're done.

items.civilians: There is no winner — end whenever the group wants or when prompts run out.
items.impostors: Rematch shuffles a fresh deck with the same settings.

presetTooltips.classic: Classic safe truths and dares with free choice each turn.
presetTooltips.sillyNight: Classic and Silly packs for a fun mix.
presetTooltips.custom: Pick your own packs, mode, and options.

spicyConfirm.message: The Spicy pack includes adult-themed truths and dares. Only continue if everyone in the group is 18+ and comfortable.

validation.emptyRoster: Add players on the Players page before starting.
validation.noPacks: Select at least one prompt pack.

play.instructions: Answer honestly or perform the dare in person — the app only provides prompts.

rules.title: How to play Truth or Dare

goal.title: Goal
goal.body: Take turns answering honest questions or performing dares. The app shows prompts — the real game happens at the table.

items.group: The group — plays together at the table while the app moderates turns.
items.player: The active player — whose turn it is to answer the truth or perform the dare.

steps.1: Choose players, packs, and prompt mode, then start.
steps.2: Each turn, the app shows whose turn it is in round-robin order.
steps.3: Pick Truth or Dare (or let the app choose in random mode), then respond at the table.
steps.4: Tap Next after the prompt is done, or Skip to pass — skips are tracked per player.
steps.5: End session anytime, or keep going until prompts run out. Rematch shuffles fresh decks.

items.civilians: There is no winner — end whenever the group wants or when prompts run out.
items.impostors: Rematch shuffles fresh decks with the same settings.

setup.emptyRoster: Add players on the Players page before starting a game.
setup.playersSubtitle: Select {min}–{max} players from your saved roster.

presetTooltips.classic: Light everyday trade-offs only — great for any group.
presetTooltips.partyMix: Classic, Gross, and Hypothetical packs together.

spicyConfirm.message: The Spicy pack includes adult-themed dilemmas. Only continue if everyone in the group is 18+ and comfortable.

validation.emptyRoster: Add players on the Players page before starting.
validation.noPacks: Select at least one dilemma pack.

play.title: Would you rather…
play.instructions: Debate as a group, then tap the option you picked to continue.

rules.title: How to play Would You Rather

goal.title: Goal
goal.body: Debate fun trade-offs as a group. The app shows two options — the real game happens at the table.

items.group: The group — debates and picks A or B verbally. No in-app voting.
items.reader: The reader — reads each dilemma aloud from the shared phone.

steps.1: Choose players and dilemma packs, then start.
steps.2: Read both options on screen aloud to the group.
steps.3: Debate and pick A or B — house rules decide how you vote.
steps.4: Tap the option the group picked to move on, or End session anytime. Rematch shuffles fresh dilemmas.

items.civilians: There is no winner — end whenever the group wants or when dilemmas run out.
items.impostors: Rematch shuffles fresh decks with the same settings.

answerTypes.duration: Answer type: a duration or amount of time

presetTooltips.classic: 1 impostor. Best for learning the rules.

validation.emptyRoster: Add players on the Players page before starting.
validation.noPacks: Select at least one question pack.
validation.noCivilians: Too many impostors — at least one civilian is required.
validation.tooManyImpostors: Too many impostors for this player count.

play.instructions: Each player answers their question aloud. Discuss, then eliminate suspects when the group agrees.

goal.title: Goal
goal.body: Civilians share one question; impostors get a different question with a similar kind of answer. Answer aloud, discuss at the table, and eliminate the impostors before they take over.

items.civilian: Civilian — sees the crew question and tries to find the impostors.
items.impostor: Impostor — sees a different question with a compatible answer type. Impostors don't know each other.

steps.1: Choose players, impostor count, and question packs, then start.
steps.2: Pass the phone — each player privately sees their question and answer type.
steps.3: Go around the table and answer your question verbally. Discuss who sounds suspicious.
steps.4: When the group agrees someone is out, eliminate them in the app. Roles stay hidden until the end.
steps.5: Keep playing until all impostors are eliminated or only impostors remain.

items.civilians: Civilians win when all impostors have been eliminated.
items.impostors: Impostors win when only impostors are still alive.

players.title: Players
players.description: Save your group's names once and reuse them in every game.
players.emptyDescription: Add the friends you play with — names are saved on this device for all games.
